glmassume 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Diagnosing GLMs with glmassume

Plot Linearity of the Logit (Deprecated)

Source: R/plot_linearity_logit.R
plot_linearity_logit.Rd

plot_linearity_logit() has been superseded by plot_linearity_link(), which works with any GLM family. This function now calls plot_linearity_link() internally.

Usage

plot_linearity_logit(model, predictors = NULL)

Arguments

model

A fitted glm object with family = binomial.

predictors

Character vector of predictor names to plot. Defaults to all numeric predictors in the model.

Value

A list of ggplot objects, one per predictor.

Examples

m <- glm(am ~ mpg + wt, data = mtcars, family = binomial)
plots <- plot_linearity_logit(m)
#> Warning: 'plot_linearity_logit' is deprecated.
#> Use 'plot_linearity_link' instead.
#> See help("Deprecated")
plots[[1]]
#> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
#> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
